zotero.abstractNote: Randomized smoothing is a state-of-the-art technique for training classifiers with provable robustness guarantees. While most existing work focuses on l2-norm robustness, l1-norm robustness is often more relevant in applications such as image classification where adversarial perturbations are constrained by the l1-norm. In this paper, we propose a new randomized smoothing approach for l1-robustness that leverages the box constraints of the input space. We show that our method significantly improves the certified radii compared to the state-of-the-art l1-smoothing methods. We provide a theoretical analysis of our approach and demonstrate its effectiveness on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 40th International Conference on Machine Learning
